LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, FEB-26-2021 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT WAG HOTELS, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WAG HOTELS, INC.'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA COURTS STRONGLY FAVOR DISPOSING OF CASES ON THEIR MERITS. (RAPPLEYEA V. CAMPBELL (1994) 8 CAL.4TH 975, 980.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-26-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff JOSEF WEIMHOLT (Tel: 773-616-9605) in pro per;
Atty. GEORGE M. WALLACE, Esq. of Wallace, Brown & Schwartz (Tel: 626-844-6777) for defendant Wag Hotels, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WAG HOTELS, INC.'s Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant Wag Hotels, Inc.'s motion to set aside entry of default is granted. California courts strongly favor disposing of cases on their merits. (Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 980.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
